name: Marketing SEO Landing Page
description: Turn a keyword + intent into a publish-ready programmatic SEO landing page for mchatai.com. Single LLM pass produces YAML frontmatter (slug, intent, meta tags, canonical, JSON-LD schema.org block, related links, CTAs, FAQ) plus the page body (hero pitch + how-to + why-mChatAI + FAQ + CTAs). Designed for the 200-pages-in-90-days programmatic-SEO push.

config.userPrompt:
You are generating one programmatic SEO landing page for mchatai.com. These pages target long-tail search intent ('build a wordle clone javascript', 'automate stripe revenue dashboard', 'daily news podcast generator'). Each page must read as a real helpful guide, not as keyword-stuffed filler. ASCII only.

Given the keyword below, do the following:

STEP A. Classify the intent. Pick ONE of:
  - build      (apps, games, mini-apps, sites, scripts the user makes)
  - automate   (recurring automations: digests, monitors, dashboards, triage)
  - make       (creative outputs: podcasts, audio, images, video)
  - recipe     (one of mChatAI's named agentic recipes)

STEP B. Derive a stable kebab-case slug from the keyword (max 60 chars, drop stop words). The full URL will be https://mchatai.com/{intent}/{slug}.

STEP C. Pick schemaType. Use one of: HowTo, SoftwareApplication, Article, Product. For 'build' default to HowTo. For 'recipe' default to HowTo. For 'automate' default to HowTo. For 'make' default to Article.

STEP D. Emit ONE complete markdown document in this EXACT format and nothing else.

Return EXACTLY this format:

---
slug: <kebab-case>
intent: <build|automate|make|recipe>
keyword: <original keyword, lowercase>
title: <SEO title tag, 50-60 chars, leads with keyword variant, no clickbait>
h1: <H1 heading for the page, differs slightly from title, includes core keyword>
metaDescription: <140-160 chars, written for click-through not keyword stuffing, includes a verb + outcome + 'mChatAI'>
metaKeywords: [<5-8 lowercase keywords, comma-separated as a YAML list>]
ogTitle: <can match title or be punchier for social>
ogDescription: <can match metaDescription>
canonical: https://mchatai.com/<intent>/<slug>
publishedAt: 2026-05-24
updatedAt: 2026-05-24
heroImage: ''
schemaType: <HowTo|SoftwareApplication|Article|Product>
relatedRecipes: [<2-4 recipe slugs you would link to, lowercase kebab-case; pick from: daily-life-organizer, text-polish-studio, web-research-agent, file-system-organizer, meeting-notes-processor, daily-news-podcast, code-review-helper, email-draft-assistant, document-summarizer, github-issue-triage, github-pr-summary, slack-channel-digest, stripe-revenue-dashboard, youtube-trend-scout, notion-knowledge-sync, discord-community-monitor, hubspot-lead-scorer, full-stack-website-builder, code-project-agent>]
relatedPosts: []
ctaPrimary:
  label: <action verb + object, max 32 chars, e.g. 'Open mChatAI and build this'>
  url: <download or deep link; for 'build' use https://mchatai.com/macos-app; for 'automate' use https://mchatai.com/macos-app; for 'recipe' use https://mchatai.com/hub; for 'make' use https://mchatai.com/applets>
ctaSecondary:
  label: <quieter alternative, e.g. 'See more recipes'>
  url: https://mchatai.com/hub
faq:
  - question: <first FAQ, 6-12 words, real searchable question>
    answer: <2-4 sentence answer, plain text, no markdown>
  - question: <second FAQ>
    answer: <answer>
  - question: <third FAQ>
    answer: <answer>
  - question: <fourth FAQ>
    answer: <answer>
---

# <h1 from frontmatter>

<Hero pitch, 70-120 words. Single paragraph. First sentence MUST include the keyword. State the outcome the visitor will achieve. End with a one-line summary of what mChatAI brings to it.>

## What you will build

<3-5 sentences describing the artifact's behavior, look, and feel. No markdown lists yet; this is the storytelling section.>

## Step by step

<5 to 8 numbered steps. Each step is one sentence describing what to do, in plain English. Reference real mChatAI surfaces by name (AIWizard, Pipelines, Recipes, Skills, Hub, mChatAIShell). Do not invent menu paths or screenshots. Each step starts with an imperative verb.>

## Why mChatAI is a good fit for this

<2-3 short paragraphs. Mention specifics: local execution, your-own-API-keys, native macOS app, hot-deploy via mchatai-source, no rebuild required. Tone: confident, not salesy. NO 'unlock', 'game-changer', 'thrilled', 'in today's world'.>

## Variations to try

<Bulleted list of 4-6 concrete variants that share the same recipe. Each bullet is 8-20 words. Aim to capture related long-tail keywords organically.>

## FAQ

<Render the FAQ from the frontmatter. Use H3 for the question and a plain paragraph for the answer. Match the frontmatter exactly.>

## Try it now

<Two-sentence call to action. First sentence states the primary action. Second sentence offers the secondary path.>

Writing rules:
- ASCII only. No smart quotes, no em-dashes (use ' - ' surrounded by spaces). No curly apostrophes.
- Do NOT invent product features, screenshots, statistics, awards, customers, version numbers.
- Do NOT include the phrases: 'unlock', 'game-changer', 'revolutionary', 'thrilled', 'we are excited to', 'in today's fast-paced world', 'level up'.
- Do NOT exceed 1500 words in the body (excluding frontmatter + FAQ).
- The keyword must appear naturally in: title, h1, metaDescription, hero pitch's first sentence, and at least one H2 section.
- The page must read as helpful to a human even if search engines vanish tomorrow.

KEYWORD:
{{INPUT}}
